examples: It was small, decayed, and rickety, but it was packed full of wretchedly poor families.
Meanwhile it is clear how wretchedly the world is failing to do so.
Four days after the Burma cyclone there is wretchedly little hard information about the victims.
This updated version, written by Daniel Pyne and Dean Georgias and directed by Jonathan Demme, is doggedly, wretchedly earnest.
It has not made the stone-breakers rich, just slightly less wretchedly poor.
